title: Four Against Darkness - Deep Dive Part 9 - Dark Waters and The Three Rings
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ZgN1aMmXELI

Daniel (@dungeondive) examines two early expansions for Four Against Darkness: Dark Waters and The Three Rings. Dark Waters is a pirate-themed adventure featuring a planned dungeon hunt for a stolen golden statue, plus a new Swashbuckler class. While the adventure itself has some structural quirks—particularly a lack of keyword systems that could otherwise track state changes in the narrative—the expansion really shines through its new charts for low-level aquatic encounters and environments. You get fresh vermin, minions, bosses, and special events perfect for theming regular games with oceanic or flooded settings.

The Three Rings, meanwhile, is a map-less adventure using D66 random encounters to build a quest around recovering stolen magical rings from orc reavers. Where Dark Waters falters narratively, The Three Rings excels in pure encounter generation, delivering exactly the kind of random-walk adventure many solo players crave. Despite the luck-dependent nature of finding clues and rings, the sheer variety of unique encounters keeps the experience fresh and story-worthy.

----

Both of these are budget-friendly expansions. Which sounds more appealing to you—the thematic adventure hook of Dark Waters or the pure random encounter structure of The Three Rings?
